title: Tracing Span and Trace Lifecycle
description: Lifecycle of traces and spans: creation, start/end events fanned out through SynchronousMultiTracingProcessor to all registered processors
trace_lifecycle_flow: TraceCtxManager calls create_trace_for_run(workflow_name, trace_id, group_id, metadata, tracing, disabled)
create_trace_for_run checks Scope.get_current_trace() — if trace already active, return None (skip creation)
IF reattach_resumed_trace=True AND trace_id was already started AND settings match → call reattach_trace() → return ReattachedTrace (no on_trace_start fired)
DefaultTraceProvider._refresh_disabled_flag(): check OPENAI_AGENTS_DISABLE_TRACING env var and manual override
IF disabled → return NoOpTrace (no processor callbacks, no-op context management)
ELSE → return TraceImpl(name, trace_id, group_id, metadata, processor=SynchronousMultiTracingProcessor)
SynchronousMultiTracingProcessor.on_trace_start(trace): iterate _processors tuple, call each processor.on_trace_start(trace), catch+log errors
WORKFLOW RUNS (spans created inside trace context)
SynchronousMultiTracingProcessor.on_trace_end(trace): iterate _processors tuple, call each processor.on_trace_end(trace), catch+log errors
span_lifecycle_flow: START: Caller uses span as context manager (e.g. `with agent_span(...) as span`)
SpanImpl.start(): check not already started, set _started_at = util.time_iso()
SynchronousMultiTracingProcessor.on_span_start(span): iterate _processors tuple, call each processor.on_span_start(span), catch+log errors
Scope.set_current_span(self) — makes this span the active span in contextvar
OPERATION RUNS (nested spans auto-inherit parent_id from Scope.get_current_span())
SynchronousMultiTracingProcessor.on_span_end(span): iterate _processors tuple, call each processor.on_span_end(span), catch+log errors
span_creation_flow: IF parent is None: resolve from Scope.get_current_span() and Scope.get_current_trace()
IF no current trace → log error, return NoOpSpan
IF current trace is NoOpTrace or current span is NoOpSpan → return NoOpSpan
parent_id = current_span.span_id (or None if root span), trace_id from current_trace
IF parent is Trace: trace_id = parent.trace_id, parent_id = None
IF parent is Span: parent_id = parent.span_id, trace_id = parent.trace_id
Return SpanImpl(trace_id, span_id, parent_id, processor=SynchronousMultiTracingProcessor, span_data, tracing_api_key, trace_metadata)

methods: on_trace_start(trace) — fan-out: for each processor call on_trace_start, catch+log Exception
on_trace_end(trace) — fan-out: for each processor call on_trace_end, catch+log Exception
on_span_start(span) — fan-out: for each processor call on_span_start, catch+log Exception
on_span_end(span) — fan-out: for each processor call on_span_end, catch+log Exception
shutdown() — fan-out: for each processor call shutdown, catch+log Exception
force_flush() — fan-out: for each processor call force_flush, catch+log Exception
fan_out_logic.error_handling: Errors in individual processors are caught and logged; other processors still execute

disabled_paths.NoOpTrace: Created when tracing is globally disabled (env or manual). No processor callbacks. Context management still works via Scope.
disabled_paths.NoOpSpan: Created when span creation has no valid parent trace, parent is NoOp, or tracing is disabled. No processor callbacks. Scope management still works.
disabled_paths.ReattachedTrace: Used on resume of an interrupted run. Does NOT fire on_trace_start — only marks trace as current in Scope.

context_management.nesting: Each span stores _prev_span_token; on finish resets to parent span. Enables automatic parent_id resolution.

provider_initialization.lazy_init: get_trace_provider() initializes DefaultTraceProvider + registers default_processor() on first access
provider_initialization.atexit: shutdown registered via atexit to flush/close processors on process exit

key_class_relationships: TraceImpl holds reference to SynchronousMultiTracingProcessor (as TracingProcessor)
SpanImpl holds reference to SynchronousMultiTracingProcessor (as TracingProcessor)
TraceCtxManager delegates to create_trace_for_run() and Trace lifecycle
TracingProcessor is abstract interface — concrete: BatchTraceProcessor, custom processors
TracingExporter is separate interface used by BatchTraceProcessor for backend export
decision_points: Is tracing globally disabled? (env OPENAI_AGENTS_DISABLE_TRACING or set_tracing_disabled) → NoOp path
Is there already an active trace in context? → Skip trace creation (use existing)
Is this a resumed trace with matching state? → ReattachedTrace (no on_trace_start)
Is parent span/trace a NoOp? → Propagate NoOp to child span
Is GeneratorExit raised in __exit__? → Skip current span/trace reset (generator cleanup)

export_format.span: { object: 'trace.span', id, trace_id, parent_id, started_at, ended_at, span_data: SpanData.export(), error, metadata? }

important_notes: All 4 lifecycle hooks (on_trace_start, on_trace_end, on_span_start, on_span_end) fire synchronously on the calling thread
Processor errors are isolated — one failing processor does not stop others
Thread safety: SynchronousMultiTracingProcessor uses a Lock for mutations; reads iterate a snapshot tuple
span_id format: 'span_<24 hex chars>'; trace_id format: 'trace_<32 hex chars>'
BatchTraceProcessor (default) batches export; call flush_traces() for immediate delivery in workers
